briefdescription.para: The type of text entry selected when on_show_keyboard is called.
Flags related to a text entry field passed when on_show_keyboard is called.
Flags to set special key states during input.

detaileddescription.para: Defines the prefix for MLWebViewResult codes.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Return values for the WebView API calls.

detaileddescription.para: The bit to enable (1) or disable (0) web inspector.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Flags set for the process using webview.

detaileddescription.para: Pause all multimedia activities of the webview.
Pause javascript timers of the webview.
Pause and discard the webview rendering process. But keep alive the MLWebView handle.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: The type of the webview pause.
The type of text entry selected when on_show_keyboard is called.
Flags related to a text entry field passed when on_show_keyboard is called.
Flags to set special key states during input.
Return values for the WebView API calls.
Flags set for the process using webview.
Struct to define the cursor's state.

detaileddescription.para: This structure must be initialized by calling MLWebViewCursorStateInit before use.

briefdescription.para: Event handler for MLWebView callbacks.

detaileddescription.para: This structure must be initialized by calling MLWebViewEventCallbacksInit before use.

briefdescription.para: Struct to define webview initialization.

detaileddescription.para: This structure must be initialized by calling MLWebViewSettingsInit before use.

briefdescription.para: Struct to define webview process initialization.

detaileddescription.para: This structure must be initialized by calling MLWebViewProcessSettingsInit before use.

briefdescription.para: The type of the webview pause.
Initializes a MLWebViewCursorState with the default parameters.

parameterdescription.para: The object to initialize with default values.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a MLWebViewEventCallbacks with the default parameters.

parameterdescription.para: The object to initialize with default settings.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a MLWebViewSettings with the default parameters.

parameterdescription.para: The object to initialize with default settings.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a MLWebViewProcessSettings with the default parameters.

parameterdescription.para: The object to initialize with default settings.

briefdescription.para: Specify the process settings to be used for webviews.

simplesect.para: Current implementation only supports enabling and disabling web inspector before first call to MLWebViewCreate. If all webviews have been destroyed, it is possible to call this with effect again. Default is to disable.

parameterdescription.para: The settings to be used for webviews.
If settings is null.
If called after MLWebViewCreate.
If settings is not null.

briefdescription.para: Create a MLWebView.

detaileddescription.para: The MLWebView will be ready to use once this function returns with MLResult_OK.

simplesect.para: In version >= 2 for MLWebViewCallbacks struct, MLWebViewCreate launches a separate thread to create a webview. If creation fails and the on_service_failed callback is not null the MLResult code will be passed into the on_service_failed callback. The on_service_connected callback will be called if it is not null and creation was successful. For version < 2 of MLWebViewCallbacks struct, MLWebViewCallbacks will create in synchronous mode.

parameterdescription.para: Handle that is ready to use with all other MLWebView API calls.
The initialization paramaters for the webview.
out_handle, in_settings, or application_vm pointer was null.

If version >= 2, returns MLResult_Ok for successful start of asynchronous operation, but any eventual failure will be reported through on_service_failed. If version < 2, this indicates that synchronous creation was successful.
Missing required permission(s).
Unable to create the MLWebView, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version < 2.

briefdescription.para: Destroy a MLWebView.

detaileddescription.para: The MLWebView will be terminated by this function call and the handle shall no longer be valid.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to invalidate.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The MLWebView was destroyed successfully.
An error occurred destroying the MLWebView.

briefdescription.para: Specify the callbacks for a MLWebView.

parameterdescription.para: The MLWebView to link the event handler.
The event handler to link to the MLWebView.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
MLWebView event handler was set.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Acquires next available frame buffer for rendering.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
Pointer to an AHardwareBuffer.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The frame is ready.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

simplesect.para: To use with OpenGL, EGL, and Vulkan please see: https://developer.android.com/ndk/reference/group/a-hardware-buffer for details.

briefdescription.para: Release a frame acquired by MLWebViewAcquireNextAvailableFrame.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
The frame being released.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
Frame successfully released.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.
An error occurred releasing the frame.

briefdescription.para: Go to a URL with the specified MLWebView.

detaileddescription.para: Note that success with this call only indicates that a load will be attempted. Caller can be notified about issues loading the URL via the event handler on_load_error.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
The URL that will be loaded.
WebView was paused. MLWebViewResume should be called before this function.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The WebView is attempting to load the specified URL.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Trigger a "Back" action in the MLWebView.

detaileddescription.para: Query MLWebViewCanGoBack before calling this method. If there is no valid page to go back to, this method will be no-op.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
The WebView Back action was initiated or cannot go back any further.
WebView was paused. MLWebViewResume should be called before this function.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Trigger a "Forward" action in the MLWebView.

detaileddescription.para: Query MLWebViewCanGoForward before calling this method. If there is no valid page to go forward to, this method will be no-op.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
WebView was paused. MLWebViewResume should be called before this function.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The WebView Forward action was initiated or cannot go forward any further.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Trigger a "Reload" action in the MLWebView.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
WebView was paused. MLWebViewResume should be called before this function.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The WebView Reload action was initiated.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Get the current URL. Set out_url to NULL to get the length of the current URL.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
The size that has been allocated in out_url to hold the URL. This will be set to the actual length of URL if inout_size and size needed for the URL are different.
The URL up to inout_size of characters.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The WebView URL was acquired.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Checks if the "Back" action is currently valid.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
True if "Back" has a valid page to go to.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The status of going "Back" was acquired.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Checks if the "Forward" action is currently valid.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
True if "Forward" has a valid page to go to.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The status of going "Forward" was acquired.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Moves the WebView mouse.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
Information about the mouse movement.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The internal mouse was moved.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Sends a mouse button down/pressed event on a specific location on screen.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
Information about the mouse button event.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
If successful.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

simplesect.para: Behavior: (1) Click behavior: On mouse down, (x, y) is saved and mouse down sent. When mouse up received within 200ms, mouse up sent to previous mouse down (x, y) position so Chromium interprets as click. (2) Drag behavior: On mouse down (x, y) is saved and mouse down sent. When mouse moves within timeout period of 200ms, webview will continue to wait. Otherwise, mouse move sent to current (x, y) location to let Chromium interpret as drag. (3) These click and drag behaviors make it possible to just send mouse events as they are received and the underlying Chromium implementation will work as expected.

briefdescription.para: Sends a mouse button up/released event on a specific location on screen.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
Information about the mouse button event.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
If successful.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Sends a printable char keyboard event to MLWebView.

parameterdescription.para: Target webview.
Printable char utf code.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The key event was injected.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Sends a key down/pressed event to MLWebView.

parameterdescription.para: Target webview.
Key code.
Should be one or combination of MLWebViewEventFlags.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The key event was injected.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Sends a key up/release event to MLWebView.

parameterdescription.para: Target webview.
Key code.
Should be one or combination of MLWebViewEventFlags.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The key event was injected.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

memberdef.argsstring: ((deprecated("Unimplemented and scheduled for removal."))) MLResult MLWebViewGetScrollSize(MLHandle web_view
memberdef.name: __attribute__

briefdescription.para: This API is stubbed out and translates to a no-op.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
The number representing the entire width of the webview, in pixels.
The number representing the entire height of the webview, in pixels.
This API is stubbed out and translates to a no-op.
The webview being accessed.
The number representing the horizontal offset of the webview, in pixels.
The number representing the vertical offset of the webview, in pixels.
This API is stubbed out and translates to a no-op.

memberdef.argsstring: (MLHandle web_view, const int32_t x_pixels, const int32_t y_pixels)
memberdef.name: MLWebViewScrollBy

briefdescription.para: Triggers a mouse "Scroll" event.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
The number of pixels to scroll on the x axis.
The number of pixels to scroll on the y axis.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The MLWebView was scrolled.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Get the current zoom factor. The default zoom factor is 1.0.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
Current numeric value for zoom factor.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The out_zoom_factor parameter was updated with the current zoom value.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.
Failed to get the zoom factor due to an internal error.

briefdescription.para: Zoom in one level.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The MLWebView zoomed in.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.
Failed to zoom in due to an internal error.
if cannot zoom in any further.

briefdescription.para: Zoom out one level.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The MLWebView zoomed out.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.
Failed to zoom out due to an internal error.
if cannot zoom out any further.

briefdescription.para: Reset zoom level to 1.0.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
The MLWebView zoom was reset.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.
Failed to reset zoom due to an internal error.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the 4x4 texture coordinate transform matrix used by all MLWebView frames.

detaileddescription.para: This transform matrix maps 2D homogeneous texture coordinates of the form (s, t, 0, 1) with s and t in the inclusive range [0, 1] to the texture coordinate that should be used to sample that location from the texture. Sampling the texture outside of the range of this transform is undefined.
The matrix is stored in column-major order so that it may be passed directly to OpenGL ES via the glLoadMatrixf or glUniformMatrix4fv functions.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
The retrieved matrix.
The constant matrix was retrieved.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.

briefdescription.para: Remove all webview cookies.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
If all cookies removed successfully.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.
Removing all cookies failed due to an internal error.

briefdescription.para: Clear the webview cache.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
WebView was paused. See MLWebViewPause.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
If cache cleared successfully.
If the MLWebView handle is not ready to use if asynchronous creation was used, MLWebViewCallbacks struct version >= 2.
Clearing cache failed due to an internal error.

briefdescription.para: Pause the webview. Call MLWebViewResume to resume.

detaileddescription.para: This method provides a multiple pause types to the webview.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.

The webview pause type MLWebViewPauseType. MLWebViewPauseType_Multimedia Is a simple pause for all multimedia of the webview. MLWebViewPauseType_Timers Intended to stop animation of the webview content. MLWebViewPauseType_Discard Uses to free the webview rendering process but keep alive the MLWebView handle.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle or invalid MLWebViewPauseType value.
If paused successfully.
MLWebView handle is not ready to use. See an asynchronous mode of the MLWebViewCreate.
Pause failed due to an internal error.

briefdescription.para: Resumes a webview after a previous call of the MLWebViewPause.

detaileddescription.para: Resume webview to the normal operation for all webview pause types.

parameterdescription.para: The webview being accessed.
Unable to find the specified MLWebView handle.
If resumed successfully.
MLWebView handle is not ready to use. See an asynchronous mode of the MLWebViewCreate.
Resume failed due to an internal error.
